note: Public summary file for Arun Verma personal website. Not raw clinical data.

variants.role: The tumor mutation that explains why IDH inhibition matters in my case.
variants.biology: IDH1 R132C changes tumor metabolism, including production of 2-HG, and is the reason IDH inhibition matters in my case.
variants.treatment: This is what connects my tumor biology to IDH inhibitor therapy: ivosidenib first, then vorasidenib as the drug I am on now.
variants.vaccine: It also matters for the exploratory vaccine story because mutation-spanning peptides can be designed around my tumor's mutations.

variants.role: One of the findings that fits with the IDH-mutant astrocytoma pattern.
variants.vaf: 69.7% VAF reported by Tempus for ATRX G1712D
variants.biology: ATRX is involved in chromatin maintenance and telomere biology; ATRX loss is common in IDH-mutant astrocytoma.
variants.treatment: Not a simple direct drug target here, but important for understanding what kind of tumor this is.
variants.vaccine: ATRX also shows up in the vaccine story because one monitored long-peptide response involved ATRX.

variants.role: I have Li-Fraumeni, so inherited TP53 risk and tumor-only TP53 findings have to be kept separate.
variants.vaf: Tumor VAF values around 49-64% appear in reports depending on assay and notation
variants.confidence: Known Li-Fraumeni context; tumor notation varies
variants.biology: TP53 is a core genome-stability gene. Because I have Li-Fraumeni, this page separates inherited TP53 risk from tumor-only TP53 findings instead of blending them together.
variants.treatment: Useful for diagnosis, surveillance, and risk framing, but I am not presenting TP53 here as a standalone treatment selector.
variants.vaccine: It could matter for mutation-spanning peptide candidates if the specific tumor alteration is confirmed and used in vaccine design.
variants.source: Germline records + BostonGene / Tempus / longitudinal notes

variants.role: MGMT promoter methylation is reported in my records.
variants.biology: MGMT promoter methylation can matter in glioma treatment discussions, especially when thinking about alkylating chemotherapy context.
variants.treatment: I treat this as treatment-context biology, not as a vaccine target or a stand-alone decision by itself.
variants.vaccine: Not a vaccine target; belongs in treatment-context biology.

variants.finding: Low TMB; MSI stable / MMR proficient
variants.role: Helps explain why I do not read this as a simple checkpoint-immunotherapy story.
variants.vaf: TMB final site summary: 1.5 mut/Mb; earlier reports showed lower assay/report-dependent estimates
variants.biology: A low mutation burden and MSI-stable profile generally means fewer classic checkpoint-response clues.
variants.treatment: This does not rule out a personalized vaccine, but it argues against any simplistic checkpoint-eligibility story.
variants.vaccine: Personalized peptide vaccines can still be designed around selected mutation-spanning peptides even in low-TMB tumors.

variants.role: CDKN2A matters in this tumor type, but I am treating this as a clue to review, not a settled conclusion.
variants.vaf: VAF not shown in the v2 card
variants.biology: CDKN2A/B status matters in IDH-mutant astrocytoma because homozygous deletion is associated with progression risk. My data summary also noted no curated cancer-gene structural variant detected for CDKN2A/CDKN2B.
variants.treatment: Useful for risk framing and for understanding why copy-number and structural-variant review matters.
variants.vaccine: Not a central vaccine target in this public summary.

variants.role: These HLA alleles shape which tumor peptide fragments might be shown to T cells.
variants.biology: Class I HLA alleles in my data were used for class-I neoantigen scoring. Class II alleles were present as helper information, but I do not currently have NetMHCIIpan class-II scoring in the cache.
variants.treatment: Important for understanding vaccine design and why HLA loss-of-heterozygosity would matter.
variants.vaccine: Top class-I candidates included HLA-B*35:01, HLA-C*03:04, HLA-C*04:01, and HLA-A*03:01 presentations.

variants.role: In my data, IDH1 R132C is the top long-peptide design candidate.
variants.biology: IDH1 R132C is both the defining tumor mutation and a mutation-spanning peptide target in the vaccine design.
variants.treatment: This is where the drug story and vaccine story meet: the same mutation matters to both.
variants.vaccine: Short epitope KPIIIGCHAY was paired with long peptide GWVKPIIIGCHAY; class-II binding score was not available in the cache.

variants.role: In my data, ATRX G1712D is one of the top long-peptide design candidates.
variants.biology: ATRX is part of the tumor identity and also appears in the vaccine-candidate list.
variants.treatment: Not a direct drug target here, but a core part of the molecular story.
variants.vaccine: Short epitope LVDPDPDFV was paired with long peptide FNKALVDPDPDFV; class-II binding score was not available in the cache.

variants.role: This is where I keep the limits visible.
variants.vaf: Tumor purity: 32%. PGx core-site call rate: 80%.
variants.biology: Some parts of the data are strong enough to explain here. Other parts need source files or orthogonal testing before I would make stronger claims.
variants.treatment: HLA LOH was blocked because tumor and normal BAMs were missing, so I would not use it to filter vaccine candidates.
variants.vaccine: Class-II/long peptide rows exist, but NetMHCIIpan class-II scoring was absent in this cache.
